review.summary: Patch 1 looks correct and fixes stale timer state. Patch 2 implements IPSR.PMIP clearing with inverted RW1C logic, so the bit would clear on writes of 0 instead of 1 and fail to clear on writes of 1.

stages.A: Scope understood: 2-patch RISC-V IOMMU series touching HPM timer state and IPSR pending-bit handling.
stages.B: Patch 1 review: resetting irq_overflow_left before recomputing overflow state is consistent with the stated bug and does not introduce an obvious regression.
stages.C: Patch 2 review: the new PMIP handling does not match RW1C semantics as described in the commit message.
stages.D: Risk assessment: incorrect PMIP clearing would break guest-visible interrupt-pending behavior and make the register semantics inconsistent with other RW1C bits.
stages.E: Conclusion: series should not be applied as-is; patch 2 needs correction.

findings.title: IPSR.PMIP RW1C handling is inverted
findings.description: The added PMIP branch clears the bit when the guest writes 0, but RW1C fields must clear when the guest writes 1. As written, a write with PMIP=1 would leave the pending bit set, while a write with PMIP=0 would clear it. That contradicts the commit message and expected IPSR semantics.
findings.suggestion: Change the condition to `if (data & RISCV_IOMMU_IPSR_PMIP) { ipsr_clr |= RISCV_IOMMU_IPSR_PMIP; }` so PMIP follows RW1C behavior like the other pending bits.
findings.confidence_reason: The patch explicitly claims RW1C support, and RW1C semantics require clearing on writes of 1. The introduced negated condition does the opposite.

disclaimer: LLM-generated draft. Not an authoritative review.
